sourceFiles.name: requirements

fix.title: Clarify cube geometry as maximum envelope and explicitly require hollow 2024 aluminium construction to reconcile mass
fix.description: This fix removes the unsatisfiable interpretation that the system is a solid 1 m-scale aluminium cube. It corrects the dimensional ambiguity in the shape requirement by defining a 1 m maximum external cube envelope, adds an explicit lightweight construction requirement stating the system is hollow and not solid, and updates the material requirement so 2024 aluminium applies to the structural shell rather than implying a fully solid aluminium body. Together these edits make the material, geometry, and weight requirements mutually consistent and directly address the review recommendation to clarify size, construction type, and lightweight rationale.

changes.description: Replaced the ambiguous and conflict-inducing shape statement with a maximum external envelope requirement using correct 3D dimensions, so the requirement no longer implies a solid 1 m-scale cube.
Scoped the material requirement to the structural shell so '2024 Aluminium' no longer implies the entire enclosed volume is solid aluminium.

Added an explicit construction requirement stating the system is hollow and thin-walled, which provides the missing lightweight construction rationale needed to reconcile aluminium material with the 1 kg mass limit.

fix.title: Add measurable geometry requirements and explicit mass-material trade resolution
fix.description: This fix resolves the finding by replacing the ambiguous shape requirement with measurable geometry requirements and by introducing an explicit conflict-resolution requirement that documents stakeholder-approved precedence and rationale for the latent conflict between geometry, material, and mass. The updated model makes the governing requirement clear: the system mass limit is mandatory, 2024 aluminium applies only if the mass limit remains satisfied, and geometry is refined to edge-length and cubeness constraints rather than the ambiguous phrase "perfect cube of 1m^2."

changes.description: Refined the ambiguous geometry requirement into a general cubic-form requirement plus measurable edge-length and orthogonality requirements, and added explicit refinement relationships.
Updated the material and weight requirement text to be implementation- and verification-ready and to align with the documented trade resolution.

Added a governing conflict-resolution requirement that documents precedence, rationale, and the approved strategy for resolving the mass/material/shape trade, then linked the affected requirements to it via refinement relationships.

results.name: Conflicting Requirements & Constraints
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: Unspecified; the model only defines three abstract system requirements about shape, material, and weight, so the intended function and user are not provided.
- Operational domain: Unspecified; no domain context such as aerospace, automotive, medical, or industrial is given.
- Lifecycle phase: Early concept / requirements definition, because the model consists only of requirement definitions with high-level textual statements and no design, behaviour, or verification content.
- Model scope: Small requirements-only fragment, not a complete system model.
- Known scope limitations: No structure, interfaces, behaviours, allocations, parametrics, operating environment, verification cases, or requirement relationships are included. Checks depending on those absent model elements are assessed as out of scope and passed only to the extent that no conflicting content is present in this fragment.

ISSUE [CONFLICT-001]
Element(s):     requirements::Material; requirements::Weight
Check:          4. Requirement-Level Conflicts
Finding:        The requirement that the system "shall be made of 2024 Aluminium" conflicts with the requirement that it "shall weigh no more than 1kg" when considered together with the specified 1 m-sized cube geometry. A cube of approximately 1 m characteristic size made from 2024 aluminium would have a mass far exceeding 1 kg, making the combined requirement set unsatisfiable unless unstated assumptions such as a hollow or lattice structure are intended.
Evidence:       requirements::Material doc: "The system shall be made of 2024 Aluminium."; requirements::Weight doc: "The system shall weigh no more than 1kg."; requirements::Shape doc: "The system shall be a perfect cube of 1m^2."
Conflict Type:  Soft
Score:          0.10 - The stated material and weight requirements, together with the given shape/size statement, strongly indicate an infeasible design space and would likely drive incorrect concept decisions.
Confidence:     MEDIUM - The conflict depends on interpreting "perfect cube of 1m^2" as a substantial 1 m-scale cube and on the reasonable engineering assumption that the system is materially composed of 2024 aluminium rather than merely containing some aluminium; the shape statement is dimensionally ambiguous.
Impact:         Concept selection and feasibility assessment are blocked because there is no credible way to satisfy the mass target with the stated material and apparent size, so downstream sizing and structural analysis would be contradictory.
Recommendation: Clarify the shape requirement with correct dimensional quantities, state whether the item is solid, hollow, or maximum envelope only, and reconcile mass versus material by relaxing the weight limit, changing material, reducing dimensions, or documenting an explicit lightweight construction rationale.

CHECK PASSED: 1. Numeric Constraint Conflicts - No explicit numeric equations, ranges, budgets, timing chains, throughput limits, power limits, or temperature constraints are modeled beyond a single mass ceiling, so no direct numeric contradiction is explicitly encoded in this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Behavioural Conflicts - The model contains no state machines, activities, transitions, triggers, sequencing rules, deadlines, or liveness/safety behaviours to compare for behavioural conflict.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Interface & Protocol Conflicts - The model defines no ports, connections, flows, message types, rates, or protocol specifications, so interface conflict checks are out of scope for this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Allocation Conflicts - No functions, components, processors, environments, or allocations are present, so allocation conflict checks cannot reveal any conflict in this model fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Safety vs. Functional Conflicts - The model contains no safety constraints, hazard controls, watchdogs, shutdown logic, or functional behaviours, so no safety-functional contradiction is represented here.

ISSUE [CONFLICT-002]
Element(s):     requirements::Material; requirements::Weight; requirements::Shape
Check:          7. Priority & Resolution
Finding:        The apparent conflict among the material, weight, and shape requirements has no documented priority ordering, rationale, refinement, or satisfaction strategy indicating which requirement governs or how the contradiction is to be resolved.
Evidence:       requirements package contains only three standalone requirement definitions with doc text and no visible «satisfy», «refine», derivation, rationale, or priority metadata: "The system shall be a perfect cube of 1m^2.", "The system shall be made of 2024 Aluminium.", and "The system shall weigh no more than 1kg."
Conflict Type:  Latent
Score:          0.25 - Once the feasibility tension is recognized, the absence of any resolution mechanism leaves stakeholders unable to determine the governing requirement or proceed consistently.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Requirement negotiation, architecture trade studies, and verification planning are undermined because different engineers could legitimately optimize for different incompatible constraints.
Recommendation: Add explicit requirement relationships or rationale showing intended precedence, refine the ambiguous geometry requirement into measurable properties, and document stakeholder-approved resolution of the mass/material/size trade.

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| CONFLICT-001 | 0.10 | MEDIUM | Material and mass requirements appear infeasible when combined with the stated cube geometry. |
| CONFLICT-002 | 0.25 | HIGH | No priority or rationale is provided to resolve the apparent requirement conflict. |

Overall Assessment Score: 0.18
